channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: From backing a ban to being hailed as a savior: Inside Trump's TikTok shift
item.description: During his first term as president, Donald Trump led the effort to ban TikTok, the hugely popular video-sharing site he said posed threats to U.S. national security.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin And Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Raucous MAGA rally punctuates events celebrating Trump on the eve of his inauguration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is using one last rally on the eve of his inauguration to again celebrate his election victory. He declared Sunday, “We won” to a crowd celebrating his return to the White House and projecting defiant optimism despite deep national political divisions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Live updates: Trump holds victory rally at Capital One Arena, TikTok restores service
item.description: President-elect Trump made the rounds rounds in Washington on Sunday and held a victory rally at Capital One Area just before Inauguration Day. Trump arrived in the nation's capital over the weekend where he is preparing to take a second term in the White House as the Republican trifecta takes form.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to take more than 200 executive actions on day one
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: President Trump will sign more than 200 executive actions on his first day in office—a massive, record-setting first wave of policy priorities focused on border security, energy, reducing the cost of living for American families, ending DEI programs across the federal government and more,
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johnson says price tag on Trump mass deportation plan is ‘small investment’ to ‘restore’ security, safety
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson said President-elect Trump’s mass deportation plan to restore safety and security to the country won't cost trillions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden pardons Virginia House Speaker Don Scott, Black nationalist Marcus Garvey
item.description: President Joe Biden pardons five people and commutes the sentence of two others who "made significant contributions to improving their communities.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Most voters in new survey seek toned-down Trump agenda
item.description: Over 60% of Americans polled disagree with Trump's idea to replace thousands of career civil-service workers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Civil rights leaders and King family mark MLK Day as a special call to action as Trump takes office
item.description: Events honoring Martin Luther King Jr.’s legacy and advocating for his vision of a just, nonviolent society will occur the same day as Donald Trump's second presidential inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden urges Americans to 'keep the faith' as he spends final full day as president in South Carolina
item.description: President Joe Biden has spent his final full day in office in South Carolina, where he urged Americans to “keep the faith in a better day to come.”.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Northern highlights: Alaska's energy, security policies are the guide feds need amid transition, group says
item.description: Future 49, with proponents from civilian pilots to Gov. Mike Dunleavy, is primed to launch to promote Alaskan issues and its energy, economic and national security benefits to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inauguration Day is tomorrow – Here’s a complete guide to Fox News Channel’s coverage and what to expect
item.description: Fox News Channel and Fox News Digital is your home to wall-to-wall coverage of President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Outgoing Interior Secretary Deb Haaland hands off closer ties with Indian Country
item.description: Deb Haaland, the country's first Indigenous cabinet secretary, used her term at the Interior Department to make what activists say is irreversible impact in recognizing the painful history of the government's treatment Native Americans.
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler, MPR News

item.title: TikTok is offline in the U.S. after Supreme Court upholds ban
item.description: The app had more than 170 million monthly users in the U.S. The black-out is the result of a law forcing the service offline unless it sheds its ties to ByteDance, its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration, a triumphant return for the Republican four years after he departed the city under the shadow of an attack by his supporters on the Capit
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pence expected to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence is planning to attend President-elect Trump’s inauguration on Monday, a source familiar with his plans said.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Governor Youngkin criticizes President Biden's clemency for officer's killers
item.description: Governor Glenn Youngkin released a statement in reference to President Biden granting clemency for two men who admitted to being responsible for the killing of a Sussex County police officer in 1998.
item.dc:creator: Chrysnel Banzouzi, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands march in anti-Trump protests just days before former president returns to power
item.description: Demonstrators rallied in St. Paul on Saturday afternoon, braving single-digit temperatures to protest ahead of President-elect Donald Trump inauguration. Trump takes the oath of office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Feven Gerezgiher and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: More Americans identify as Republican: Gallup poll
item.description: It is the third consecutive year the GOP has claimed the majority; a shift during the Trump era of American politics.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Mass deportation raids could start in Chicago as soon as Tuesday: Report
item.description: Mass deportation raids threatened in Chicago could start as soon as Tuesday, according to a report from the Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, NewsNation

item.title: Free upgrades and pardon requests: What it's like to share a name with a president
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There’s not only one Donald Trump in the United States. Nor is there only one William J. Clinton. Plenty of people share names with famous presidents. Donald Trump, a veteran and resident of Alabama, said because of his name he often is gifted things for free. William J.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Merrick Garland exits with his record under scrutiny and the Justice Department bracing for upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — During hearings on Merrick Garland's nomination to be President Joe Biden's attorney general, the longtime federal appeals court judge told senators in 2021 that he hoped to “turn down the volume” on the public discourse about the Justice Department and return to the days when the
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The inauguration is moving indoors. Here's what to know about the changed plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Sub-freezing temperatures forecast for Monday in Washington are prompting President-elect Donald Trump to take the oath of office from inside the Capitol Rotunda. Almost everyone visiting Washington won’t be able to see it in person.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: The 2025 Legislature is off to a bumpy start
item.description: DFLers made good on their threat to keep the Minnesota House in limbo earlier this week as both parties fought over control. Republicans pressed forward anyway. MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guests talk about the unconventional start to the 2025 legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Texas lawmakers urge Trump's Secretary of State pick to get tough with Mexico on water payments
item.description: Several Texas lawmakers have asked President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee for Secretary to State to help urge Mexico to pay the water it owes the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: What’s next for Kamala Harris
item.description: Inauguration Day marks the first time in more than 20 years that Kamala Harris will not be in public office. “It is not my nature to go quietly into the night,” she told allies on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences of nearly 2,500 non-violent drug offenders
item.description: Biden said he is proud of his record on clemency and will “continue to review additional commutations and pardons” before he leaves office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How would RFK Jr. handle bird flu? His record on vaccines has experts on edge
item.description: Trump's pick for health secretary has a record of attacking vaccines. In fact he's even taken direct aim at bird flu shots. Some scientists fear this doesn't bode well in case of a crisis.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota courts may get final say in government's constitutional clash
item.description: An ugly opening to Minnesota’s legislative session and a campaign season that won’t end is fueling lawsuit after lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis outlines immigration plan for lawmakers to consider ahead of special session
item.description: During a stop at the Polk County Sheriff’s Office, Gov. Ron DeSantis proposed a list of statewide immigration measures ahead of a special session.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers address red tape holding residents back from rebuilding after storms
item.description: The blame game continues on the local, state, and federal levels as officials discuss who can be held accountable for hurricane relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with explosion of online porn
item.description: “Kids can get online porn through gaming systems, tablets, phones, computers. Let me just say that content filtering for all those different devices, I can say from personal experience, is difficult to keep up with,” said Justice Amy Coney Barrett, who has seven children.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada attorney general moves to protect health care access for DACA recipients
item.description: Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford is intervening to protect access to health care insurance for Dreamers — the recipients of the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) policy.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DeSantis hopes incoming Trump administration will kickstart crackdown on illegal immigration
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis will be speaking in Winter Haven on Wednesday morning at the Polk County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Few US adults confident Justice Department and FBI will act fairly under Trump, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: As President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general, Pam Bondi, prepares for questioning from senators on Wednesday, only about 2 in 10 U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: County Board of Supervisors votes to hold special election to fill District 1 seat
item.description: In a swift and unanimous vote, the County Board of Supervisors has agreed to a special election to fill the vacancy left behind by Nora Vargas.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Vara, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Democrats turn up heat on Hegseth as Republican alleges hypocrisy
item.description: Defense Secretary nominee Pete Hegseth appeared before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Tuesday, the first of President-elect Trump's Cabinet candidates to face confirmation.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis's call for special session 'premature,' Senate President, House Speaker say
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a special legislative session to address illegal immigration, among other issues, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC
